scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every documented REST endpoint from Chameleon's own Developer Hub (https://developers.chameleon.io/llms.txt index and its per-resource /apis/*.md reference pages: surveys, tours, launchers, tooltips, segments, embeds, event_names, tags, webhooks, deliveries, profiles/profiles-search, companies, demos, steps, elements) is now either a stream, a writes.json action, or excluded with a specific reason. This research also corrected a wave2 defect: the prior bundle assumed offset/limit pagination with no real-docs backing (legacy's own code comment admits "no documented next-page token"); the real API documents cursor pagination via limit/before/after with a cursor.before token in the response body, now reflected in streams.json's base.pagination.

excluded.reason: single-Microsurvey detail is a strict subset of the surveys list stream's fields
single-Tour detail is a strict subset of the tours list stream's fields
single-Launcher detail is a strict subset of the launchers list stream's fields
single-Tooltip detail is a strict subset of the tooltips list stream's fields
single-Embeddable detail is a strict subset of the embeds list stream's fields
single-Segment detail is a strict subset of the segments list stream's fields

lists Experiences (tours/surveys/launchers) already attached to one Segment; every returned Experience is already present, with the same fields, in its own top-level stream (tours/surveys/launchers) — this is a filtered join view, not new data
single-Event-Name detail is a strict subset of the event_names list stream's fields
single-Tag detail is a strict subset of the tags list stream's fields

applies a heterogeneous array of tag add/remove/set operations across mixed model_type targets (Campaign/Tooltip/List) in one call; the declarative write dialect's fixed record_schema-per-action shape cannot express a variable-shaped, mixed-target batch operation without a Tier-2 WriteHook, and no single per-record-type tag write exists yet to compound instead

updates an existing pending Delivery's targeting/timing fields; create_delivery already covers the primary trigger-an-experience mutation and an update is only possible before the delivery fires (at is null), a narrow follow-up edit deferred pending real demand

POST variant of the same company-search listing the companies stream already covers via GET with no filters; the POST form exists only to carry a large/complex filters body, out of scope until a caller needs filtered search rather than a full list
single-Company detail by id is a strict subset of the companies list stream's fields
single-Company lookup by uid query param; same data already reachable via the companies list stream
irreversibly deletes a Company record (optionally cascading to all associated user profiles); out of scope for this connector's conservative non-destructive write surface
same irreversible company deletion, addressed by uid instead of id

single-profile lookup by id/uid/email; no plain unfiltered list variant is documented (only /analyze/profiles search, itself excluded below), so there is no natural stream to attach a detail-duplicate exclusion to
singular alias of /analyze/profiles/:id for uid/email lookup; same narrow single-record read

search-only endpoint requiring a filters/segment_id body to scope results (no plain "list everyone" default documented, unlike /analyze/companies which explicitly supports an unfiltered list); the response example shown in the docs also omits the cursor object every other list endpoint returns, so pagination behavior for the unfiltered case is not confidently documented — a SCHEMA_AMBIGUOUS pagination shape, not a data-shape one
same search-endpoint scoping and undocumented-pagination limitation as the GET form
returns a single aggregate count integer, not enumerable per-record rows with a primary key
POST form of the same aggregate count endpoint
irreversibly clears/resets a User Profile's tracked data (browser info, Microsurvey responses, event history); out of scope for this connector's conservative write surface
same irreversible profile-clearing mutation, addressed by uid query param instead of id

permanently deletes a User Profile and all associated data for privacy/opt-out compliance; genuinely destructive and out of scope for this connector's write surface (a data-subject-request workflow belongs in a dedicated privacy-ops tool, not an ordinary sync connector)
same permanent profile deletion, addressed by uid query param instead of id

Chameleon's Product Demos reference page (/apis/demos) documents only the Demo/DemoRun/DemoSubmission/DemoFormField schemas and CRM-sync behavior; it contains no HTTP Request section for a list/retrieve REST endpoint at all (Demos are recorded via the Chrome extension and consumed via CRM sync or outbound Demo webhooks, not a queryable REST list) — there is no endpoint to implement

Steps (/apis/steps) are documented as embedded objects within Tours/Microsurveys/Embeddables, retrievable only via an experience's own expand param — there is no standalone /edit/steps list/detail endpoint to implement

Elements (/apis/elements) are documented as embedded objects within Steps/Tours only, with explicit text stating "they are not listed or retrieved independently" — there is no standalone endpoint to implement
